reason_summary: Single wire transfer of $450,000 to high-risk offshore entity in Cayman Islands (CP_017).
Multiple cash deposits totaling $12,500 within 5 days. Resolved: verified sale of personal vehicle with supporting bill-of-sale documentation provided by customer via secure messaging portal.
Received $200,000 from Switzerland and immediately wired out $198,500 to Panama shell company within 2 hours.
Five cash deposits of exactly $9,800 or $9,900 within a 7-day window, avoiding the $10,000 CTR reporting threshold.

Account inactive for 180 days suddenly received a wire of $85,000. Verified: distribution from a mature estate trust with accompanying probate documentation submitted by customer's legal representative.

Monthly transactional turnover of $42,000 exceeds expected monthly turnover of $5,000. Customer is a retail-segment individual with no prior high-value transaction history. Escalated pending source-of-funds verification.

Screening match detected: beneficial-ownership registry links CUST_018 to Apex Import Export Holdings Ltd, which appears on the OFAC SDN List (HIT_004, match score 1.0). Monthly service fee payment of GBP 15,000 routed to Belize (CP_001) flagged as potential sanctions evasion.

High volume of small-value online purchases matching typical e-commerce activity. Rule R-112 (Retail Baseline Velocity) confirmed all transactions within expected frequency and value bands for segment.

Wire of EUR 120,000 sent to Belize-registered entity (CP_002). Alert downgraded from HIGH to MEDIUM following review: customer CUST_016 is an established import/export SMB with a documented Belize supplier relationship. Original invoice AP-2098, customs declarations, and bill of lading verified by trade-finance team within 4 hours of alert creation. Rule R-221 (Verified Trade Counterparty Exemption) applied. Auto-cleared per Level-1 analyst sign-off.
Multiple high-value ATM withdrawals over the weekend. Resolved: Checked against customer's prior vacation travel notice. Rule R-089 (Pre-notified Travel Cash Exemption) applied.

Received wire of GBP 35,000 which is 5x monthly salary. Resolved: Verified as tuition assistance from grandparents via accompanying bank confirmation letter. Rule R-045 (Verified Third-Party Gift) applied.

Three cash deposits of EUR 9,500 over 4 days. Alert downgraded from HIGH to MEDIUM: customer CUST_012 provided event-organiser receipts and cash-intake reconciliation sheets from a two-day hobbyist collectibles fair. Total cash received from fair stall sales EUR 28,500; deposits represent partial bank-ins consistent with the documented event schedule. Rule R-134 (Documented Cash-Intensive Event Exemption) applied by Level-1 analyst.

Merchant settlement volume spiked by 40% due to Black Friday/Seasonal promotion. Rule R-055 (Seasonal Merchant Volume Variance) confirmed activity within pre-approved promotional period parameters. Legitimate business activity.

scenario_name: Dormant Account Reactivated by Cash Out
reason_summary: Account inactive for 210 days initiated a cash withdrawal of $10,000. Verified: customer presented government-issued ID in branch; withdrawal purpose confirmed as home renovation contractor payment. Rule R-067 (Branch-Verified Reactivation) applied.

Outgoing wire of $95,000 to Belize counterparty (CP_010) matches adverse-media allegations of overseas corruption and asset concealment involving customer CUST_005 (HIT_002 confirmed; HIT_005 newly identified source). Combined with confirmed PEP status (HIT_001) and prior large Cayman wire (TXN_0047), pattern is consistent with multi-jurisdiction layering.

Funds of $150,000 received from offshore Swiss entity (CP_011) and $149,000 forwarded to Dubai supplier (CP_013) within 48 hours. While trade-financing documentation was submitted, the near-identical round-trip amounts and 99.3% pass-through rate exceed Level-1 clearance thresholds. Escalated to L2 for enhanced counterparty due diligence and beneficial ownership verification.

scenario_name: High-Value Wire to High Risk Geo
reason_summary: Wire of GBP 75,000 sent to high-risk geography counterparty (CP_008, Seychelles nominee). Transaction volume exceeds monthly expected turnover for CUST_017. Invoice and customs declaration submitted for review; escalated pending independent verification of machinery purchase.
